title: Storage, Filesystems, Archives, and Backups · Transfer Runbook
summary: Respond to low disk space and protect service data without confusing filesystems, mounts, archives, snapshots, and verified backups. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a storage map and restore-tested backup plan with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Relate block devices, partitions, filesystems, mount points, namespaces, and directory visibility.
Distinguish apparent size, allocated blocks, free space, inodes, deleted-open files, and per-directory usage.
Use lsblk, findmnt, mount, and filesystem identifiers to observe topology before any mount or format action.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
lab case — Filesystem / is 92% full with 500 free inodes while a deleted-open log consumes 1.4 GiB. Build an archive and restore proof without formatting, mounting, or deleting host data.

What must be established before implementation?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for relate block devices, partitions, filesystems, mount points, namespaces, and directory visibility.
